title: impl: chat_typing, chat_query_changes
description:
What: Implement chat_typing and chat_query_changes methods on JmapChatClient in methods/chat.rs.

Algorithm:
1. chat_typing(&self, session, chat_id: &str, typing: bool) -> Result<super::TypingResponse, ClientError>:
   a. Guard: if chat_id.is_empty() { return Err(ClientError::InvalidArgument("chat_typing: chat_id must not be empty".into())) }
   b. let (api_url, account_id) = Self::session_parts(session)?;
   c. let args = json!({accountId: account_id, chatId: chat_id, typing: typing})
   d. let (call_id, req) = super::build_request("Chat/typing", args)
   e. let resp = self.call(api_url, &req).await?
   f. crate::client::extract_response(resp, call_id)
2. chat_query_changes(&self, session, since_query_state: &str, max_changes: Option<u64>) -> Result<super::QueryChangesResponse, ClientError>:
   a. let (api_url, account_id) = Self::session_parts(session)?;
   b. let mut args = json!({accountId: account_id, sinceQueryState: since_query_state})
   c. if let Some(mc) = max_changes { args["maxChanges"] = mc.into(); }  // omit when None (Pattern B)
   d. let (call_id, req) = super::build_request("Chat/queryChanges", args)
   e. call + extract_response

Note: chat_query_changes mirrors message_query_changes exactly (message.rs:233-250). TypingResponse is imported from super::.

Defensive programming rules:
- empty chat_id guard fires before session_parts (no network call)
- max_changes: omit when None (not null) — same as message_query_changes
- No unwrap(), no key material

Acceptance: cargo test passes; chat_typing and chat_query_changes exist on JmapChatClient; clippy and fmt clean
acceptance_criteria: cargo test passes; chat_typing and chat_query_changes exist on JmapChatClient; clippy and fmt clean
close_reason: chat_typing (with empty-id guard) and chat_query_changes implemented in chat.rs
